msItem.title: Gadla samāʿtat from Maskaram to Naḥase
On (the Life and Decollation of) John the Baptist
Martyrium of Mammes, Theodotus and his wife Rufina
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddus liqa ḥarā Fāsiladas
Martyrium of Eustathius, his wife and his two children
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddus Qoṗrǝyānos wa-qǝddǝst ʾIyusṭā
Passio of Mercurius, recension a, b, c
Zenāhomu la-sabʾa Nāgrān wa-sǝmʿomu la-qǝddus Ḫirut wa-ʾǝlla mǝslehu
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-Ṗeṭros liqa ṗāṗṗāsāt za-ʾƎlla ʾƎskǝndǝryā
Zenā la-qǝddǝst ʾAwgānyā wa-zenā ʾabu-hā qǝddus Filǝṗṗos
Gadlu la-qǝddus samāʿt wa-ḫayyāl wa-mastagādǝl ʾAnqiṭos wa-ʾǝlla mǝslehu
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddus Marmǝhnām walda Sanāḥereb nǝguśa ʾAtor wa-za-ʾǝḫǝtu Sārā
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddus Tewodros Banādlewos za-hagara ʾAnṣokiyā
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddus wa-kǝbur samāʿt nǝṣuh ʾabbā Pifāmon
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddǝst Ṭeqalā wa-za-4-danāgǝl ʾǝlla mǝsle-hā
Martyrdom of the forty soldiers of Cappadocia
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddus Yoḥannǝs walda ṣarābi
Discourse on Victor, by Cyriacus, bishop of Behnesa
msItem.incipit: ድርሳን፡ ዘደረሰ፡ አቡነ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ስቡሕ፡ ወቡሩክ፡ በኵሉ፡ ምግባራት፡ አባ፡ ሕርያቆስ፡ ኤጲስ፡ ቆጶስ፡ ዘሀገረ፡ ብሕንሳ፡ ባሕሎ፡ በእንተ፡ ስባሔ፡ ወክብረ፡ መስፍን፡ ጽኑዕ፡ ወዓቢይ፡ መኰንን፡ ኅሩይ፡ ለባሴ፡ መዊእ፡ ክቡር፡ ሰማዕት፡ ቅዱስ፡ ድንግል፡ ማር፡ ፊቅጦር፡ ወልደ፡ ኅርማኖስ፡ መኰንን።

msItem.title: Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddus wa-kǝbur Yoḥannǝs za-Sǝnhut
Martyrium of the soldiers Arsenofis, Peter and Askirion
Sǝmʿu la-qǝddus samāʿt ḫayyāl tǝguh ʾAbakarazun
Gadla rǝʾǝsa nagaśtāt Yosṭos wa-ʾAboli waldu wa-Tāwǝklǝyā bǝʾsitu
Sǝmʿ za-śalās danāgǝl za-ba Rome ʾǝlla ʾasmātihon Ṗisṭis wa-ʾAlāṗis wa-ʾAgāṗis
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddǝst wa-bǝḍǝʿt Makbǝyu wa-7daqiqā
Zenāhu la-qǝddus wa-buruk Ḥarusṭǝfārus wa-kǝlʾe qǝddusāt ʾanǝst
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddus ʾƎnṭāwos
msItem.incipit: አአኵቶ፡ ለእግዚአብሔር፡ ዘመሐረኒ፡ ውስተ፡ ርስተ፡ ወልዱ፡ ኢየሱስ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ እንዘ፡ ውስተ፡ ጽልመት፡ ሀሎኩ። እስመ፡ ብእሲ፡ ፈረሳዊ፡ አነ፡ ወብሔርየ፡ ቀራይስ፡ ወስምየ፡ እንጣዎስ፡ እምዘርአ፡ አሞሬዎን። ወእንዘ፡ ለንጉሠ፡ ምድር፡ እትቀነይ፡ ኮንኩ፡ ሕፁፀ፡ አእምሮ፡ እንዘ፡ አኃሥሥ፡ ንዋየ፡ ዓለም፡ ተበዊሕየ፡ ከመ፡ ይሑር፡ ሀገረ፡ ደማስቆ። ወሶበ፡ ሰምዑ፡ በእንቲአየ፡ ፈርሁ፡ ወተሀውከት፡ ኵለ፡ ሀገረ፡ ደማስቆ፡ ወዓርጉ፡ ዲበ፡ ናሕስ፡ ወነገሩኒ፡ ከመ፡ ሀለዉ፡ ውስተ፡ ቤተ፡ ክርስቲያነ፡ ቴዎድሮስ፡ ሰማዕት።

msItem.title: Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddus ʾƎndǝryānos wa-za-20wa4 samāʿtāt wa-za-bǝḍǝʿt ʾƎnṭolyā
Martyrium of Irenaeus, Bishop of Sirmium

supportDesc.foliation: Foliation by the library in the upper right corner. Two unnumbered blank leaves each at the beginning and end, both referred to as in the library's digitization. The first and last leaf are not mentioned by Wright and were possibly added by the library.

collation.signatures: Quire marks are written on the first folio, in the upper left corner of each quire, i.e. quires 1-30. On the digital images, they are not visible; because covered by the paper strips.

list.item: 1 s.l., no stub Folio not foliated.
፲፮ 2 1, stub after stub after 2; 2, stub after 2

supportDesc.condition: The manuscript is generally in a good condition: a few leaves are covered by stains (therefore some passages of the text are not readable), e.g. , , .

layout.ab: Primary pricks and horizontal pricks are visible.
The upper line is written above the ruling, the lower line is written above the ruling. The first and last folios are not ruled. On and the ruling lines are not completed.
layout.note: The text dimensions, with margins, are taken from

handNote.desc: Written in a fine hand. Cues (letters; in black ink) on the left margin or internal margin of a few folios, e.g. , , .
handNote.seg: Divine names. A few lines (grouped in two) on the first folio (); groups of lines at the incipit and at incipit page of each text. Names of the saints protagonists; the name of the king ኢዮአስ፡ ʾIyoʾas, of the father ኢያሱ ʾIyāsu (i.e.ʾIyāsu II), of the queen ወለተ፡ ጊዮርጊስ፡ Walatta Giyorgis, and of ወለተ፡ ሥላሴ፡ Walatta Śǝllāse, and ʾƎleniእሌኒ፡ (within the supplication formulas; see e.g. ). Elements of the punctuations signs and of the quire marks. Marginal notes on the upper murgins.

item.desc: The note states that the manuscript belongs to the church of Madḫāne ʿĀlam.
The name of the scribe was Walda Fasiladas.

The manuscript was written during the reign of ʾIyoʾas, son of ʾIyāsu II. His name appears in all supplications, together with those of dowager queen Walatta Giyorgis, and either ʾƎleni or Walatta Śǝllāse.

binding.decoNote: In the original boards, covered with blind-tooled leather and lined with flowered silk.

Six pairs of sewing stations. The threads are white, not possible to understand whether artificial (from the library) or original (parchment or vegetarian). The centrefold of the manuscript is not fully visible; the manuscript is huge and it is not possible to open it completely.

history.provenance: The manuscript had very probably belonged to a church in the Gondar area, from where it was taken by Tewodros and brought to Madḫāne ʿĀlam. It was then looted by the British Napier expedition in 1868.

bibl.title: Catalogue of the Ethiopic Manuscripts in the British Museum Acquired since the Year 1847

Biblia Veteris Testamenti Aethiopica, in quinque tomos distributa, ad librorum manuscriptorum fidem edidit et apparatu critico instruxit Dr. A.D., Professor Tubingensis. Veteris Testamenti Aethiopici Tomus Primus, sive Octateuchus Aethiopicus. Ad librorum manuscriptorum fidem edidit et apparatu critico instruxit Dr. A. D., Professor Tubingensis, Impensarum partem suppeditante Societate Germanorum Orientali
bibl.publisher: Sumptibus Fr. Chr. Guil. Vogelii, Typis Guil. Vogelii, Filii

projectDesc.p: Encoded according to TEI P5 Guidelines.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

t:revisionDesc.change: Updated dates, keywords and catalogue range

Added collation; added rubrication; added textual dimensions, with margins; updated outer dimensions; updated binding description, ruling and condition; added cues to the hand description. The data were collected directly at the British Library, on the days 23 and 24 of Febrary 2023, by Vitagrazia Pisani, within the frame work of the DFG Project 'The Gǝʿǝz Version of the Passio of St Cyricus (Gadla Qirqos): A Critical Edition, Translation, and Commentary', project number: 468455109 (Principal investigator: Vitagrazia Pisani
